title: blechnum auratum subsp. columbiense
intro: Blechnum auratum subsp. columbiense, also known as the Columbian golden fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. It is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in South America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Blechnum auratum subsp. columbiense include its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections due to its antimicrobial properties and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into a tea, infused in water, or used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
